ErrorResponse.description:
Standard error envelope returned by the signing endpoint when a request
fails validation or an upstream dependency errors. The shape is
intentionally minimal: a single human-readable string ErrorResponse.Error field.

error.description: A human-readable description of what went wrong.

OverlayType.description: The type of overlay to render onto a PDF page.

PdfAttachment.description:
A file embedded in the PDF as a PDF/A-3 associated file. The file
content is base64-encoded so the entire signing payload can travel
over JSON without multipart bodies.

filename.description: Display name of the file as shown in PDF viewers.

oneOf.description:
PDF/A-3 associated-file relationship. Defaults to
AfRelationship.Source when omitted.

PdfRenderFinalizeRequest.description:
Request body for the `POST /api/render-signed/finalize` endpoint.
Injects the externally-computed CMS signature into the prepared PDF's
reserved placeholder and returns the final PAdES-signed PDF/A-3 document.

algorithm.description:
Signature algorithm identifier. Currently only
`"ecdsa.p256.sha256"` is supported.

certificatePem.description:
PEM-encoded X.509 certificate of the signer. Must be the same
certificate used during prepare. Re-sent here so the finalize
endpoint is fully stateless.

preparedPdfBase64.description:
The prepared PDF blob as returned by
`POST /api/render-signed/prepare`. Must be passed back unchanged.

signatureBytes.description:
Base64-encoded ECDSA signature over the
`digestToSignBase64` returned by prepare.

signatureFieldName.description: Signature field name to fill (must match the value used during prepare).

timestampToken.description:
Optional base64-encoded RFC 3161 timestamp token computed over
`SHA-256(signatureBytes)`. When present, the timestamp is
embedded as an unsigned attribute in the CMS signer info,
enabling Adobe Reader to display the trusted timestamp in the
signature panel.

PdfRenderPrepareRequest.description:
Request body for the `POST /api/render-signed/prepare` endpoint.
Carries everything needed to render an HTML document into a PDF/A-3B
archive, embed associated files, render a visible signature block on
an appended last page, and reserve a fixed-size signature placeholder
suitable for offline ECDSA signing.

attachments.description:
Files to embed in the PDF as PDF/A-3 associated files. Typically
the canonical JSON payload that was cryptographically signed,
allowing offline verification of the document's content against
the embedded JSON.

certificatePem.description:
PEM-encoded X.509 certificate of the signer. Required at prepare
time because the CMS signed-attributes structure (whose digest the
caller signs) binds the certificate's issuer and serial number.
The same certificate must be re-sent in the finalize request.

hashAlgorithm.description:
Optional hash algorithm identifier. Currently only `"SHA-256"`
is supported. Defaults to `"SHA-256"`.

html.description:
HTML document body to render. The HTML is converted to PDF/A-3B
using iText pdfHTML. Standard semantic HTML and inline CSS are
supported. JavaScript and external network resources are ignored.

signatureFieldName.description:
Optional name of the signature field in the resulting PDF.
Defaults to `"Signature1"`.

signatureFieldSize.description:
Optional size in bytes of the reserved signature placeholder.
Defaults to `16384`, which accommodates a P-256 ECDSA
signature plus an X.509 certificate plus an RFC 3161 timestamp
token plus DER overhead. Increase if finalize fails with
"signature container too large".

visibleSignatureBlock.description:
Metadata for the visible signature block rendered on a new page
appended to the document.

PdfRenderPrepareResponse.description:
Successful response from `POST /api/render-signed/prepare`.
Contains the prepared PDF (with a reserved signature placeholder)
and the digest the caller must ECDSA-sign before calling finalize.

byteRange.description:
The four `/ByteRange` integers from the prepared PDF
(`[offset1, length1, offset2, length2]`). Returned for
diagnostic and verification purposes only — the caller does not
need them for finalize.

byteRangeDigestBase64.description:
Base64-encoded SHA-256 digest of the bytes covered by
IReadOnlyList<long> PdfRenderPrepareResponse.ByteRange. Returned for diagnostic purposes only.
This is NOT the value the caller signs (see
string PdfRenderPrepareResponse.DigestToSignBase64).

digestToSignBase64.description: Base64-encoded SHA-256 digest of the DER-encoded CMS signed attributes. The caller signs this with ECDSA, not the PDF byte range directly. The signed attributes contain the byte-range digest as their `messageDigest` attribute, so signing this value transitively binds the PDF content.

preparedPdfBase64.description:
The prepared PDF as a base64-encoded byte string. The caller must
pass this back unchanged to `/api/render-signed/finalize`.

signatureFieldName.description:
Echoed signature field name (defaults to `"Signature1"`).
Pass it back to finalize to ensure the right field is filled.

PdfVisibleSignatureBlock.description:
Metadata describing the visible signature block rendered on a new
page appended to the document. All fields except
string? PdfVisibleSignatureBlock.CertificateNumber are required.

attestationText.description:
Free-form attestation paragraph describing how the document can be
verified (e.g., "This record was electronically signed and can be
verified in Adobe Acrobat Reader...").

certificateNumber.description:
Optional credential or certificate number identifying the signer
(e.g., FAA airman certificate number).

dateTime.description:
Local date and time of the signing event with timezone offset.
Rendered for human readability; the cryptographic timestamp
(when present) is the authoritative time anchor.

intentText.description:
Free-form intent statement displayed to the signer at signing
time and rendered into the visible block on the PDF.

keyFingerprint.description:
Hex-encoded fingerprint of the signing public key. Rendered in
monospaced type so a verifier can compare it character by character
against an external trust store.

signerName.description: Full legal name of the signer.

signerRole.description: Role or title of the signer in the signing context.

SignPdfRequest.description:
Request body for the `POST /api/sign` endpoint. Contains a base PDF
and a collection of overlay instructions describing how signature elements
should be rendered onto the document.

basePdfBase64.description: The base PDF document to apply overlays to, encoded as a base64 string.

overlays.description:
One or more overlay instructions to render onto the PDF. Overlays are
applied in the order supplied, which determines their z-order.

SignPdfResponse.description:
Successful response from `POST /api/sign`. Contains a short-lived
presigned URL that the caller can use to download the completed PDF
directly from AWS S3.

downloadUrl.description:
A presigned HTTPS URL pointing to the rendered PDF in S3. This URL is
time-limited and grants `GET` access only. Anyone holding the URL
can download the document until it expires.

expiresAtUtc.description:
UTC timestamp at which DownloadUrl stops being valid.
The TTL is controlled by the `PRESIGNED_URL_TTL_MINUTES` environment
variable and defaults to 60 minutes.

SignatureOverlay.description: A single overlay element to render at a specific location on a PDF page.

fontFamily.description:
Optional font family to use for text overlays. Defaults to
`Helvetica`. Only standard PDF font names are guaranteed to work
across environments (`Helvetica`, `Times-Roman`, `Courier`,
and their bold/italic variants).

fontSize.description: Optional font size in PDF points. Defaults to `12`.

height.description: Height of the overlay bounding box in PDF points. Must be greater than zero.

imageBase64.description:
Required when OverlayType SignatureOverlay.Type is OverlayType.SignatureImage.
A base64-encoded PNG or JPEG image. Ignored for other overlay types.

pageNumber.description: The 1-based page number in the base PDF to apply this overlay to.

text.description:
Required when OverlayType SignatureOverlay.Type is OverlayType.Text. For
OverlayType.DateStamp this is an optional custom
DateTime format string (defaults to `MM/dd/yyyy`).
Ignored for OverlayType.SignatureImage.

type.description:
The kind of overlay to render. Determines which of
string? SignatureOverlay.Text or string? SignatureOverlay.ImageBase64 is required.

width.description: Width of the overlay bounding box in PDF points. Must be greater than zero.

x.description: Horizontal position in PDF points, measured from the left edge of the page.

y.description: Vertical position in PDF points, measured from the bottom edge of the page.

get.description: Returns the service name and a static running status. Useful for quick sanity checks.

post.description: Step 2 of 2 in the stateless deferred-signing protocol. Accepts the prepared PDF blob from /prepare along with the externally-computed ECDSA signature bytes, the signer's X.509 certificate, and an optional RFC 3161 timestamp token. Builds the CMS SignedData via iText's PdfPKCS7, injects it into the reserved placeholder, and returns the final PAdES-B-B PDF/A-3 document as application/pdf bytes for the caller to archive.
post.summary:
Inject the externally-computed CMS signature into the prepared PDF
and return the final PAdES-signed PDF/A-3 bytes.

post.description: Step 1 of 2 in the stateless deferred-signing protocol. Renders the supplied HTML to PDF/A-3B, embeds attachments as PDF/A-3 associated files, renders a visible signature block on an appended last page, reserves a fixed-size signature placeholder via iText's PdfSigner, and returns the prepared PDF (base64) plus the SHA-256 digest of the CMS signed attributes that the caller must ECDSA-sign with their private key. The service holds NO state between this call and finalize.
post.summary:
Render HTML to PDF/A-3B, embed attachments, render the visible
signature block, reserve a signature placeholder, and return the
digest the caller must ECDSA-sign.

post.description:
Accepts a base64-encoded PDF and a list of overlay instructions
(signature images, text, date stamps). The service composites the
overlays onto the document using iText, uploads the result to AWS S3,
and returns a short-lived presigned HTTPS URL the caller can use to
download the signed PDF. The service performs NO cryptographic
signing — all signature data is supplied externally.

requestBody.description: Cancellation token for the HTTP request.

post.summary: Render signature overlays onto a PDF and return a presigned download URL.
